title: Validate 4-node mesh on the wired switch (real babeld + client data path)
description: Bring up all 4 nodes on the shared wired switch with the new LAN-mode/IPv4-backhaul build. Apply container-net-lan.rsc ($meshLink = switch-facing port) on each, deploy the fresh image, run 'mesh' (defaults --lan) with a roster of all 4 ids, REAL babeld (not stubbed). Validate: (a) each node assigns its 10.254 backhaul; (b) all 6 pairs form kind=DIRECT tunnels (path logger); (c) CRDT converges, 4 distinct /24 claims; (d) babeld propagates all /24s; (e) a client on node A's LAN reaches a host on node B's LAN across the mesh. This is the hardware proof of the 4pk mechanism before the wireless backhaul swap. Blocks the wireless-backhaul work (prove software on the wire first).

title: LAN-mode backhaul: container on shared L2 + IPv6 link-local node discovery
description: Pivot the same-site mesh off the relay/double-NAT path. (1) Put the meshd container on the shared L2 segment (bridge veth-mesh to the switch-facing port) so peers are mutually reachable instead of buried behind each node's masquerade. (2) Default the daemon to --lan (mDNS + relays disabled, fully offline). (3) Node-to-node backhaul addressing via IPv6 link-local (zero-config, collision-free, already proven for babeld in op4), with a node-id-derived IPv4 fallback if iroh mDNS can't dial link-local. Unifies the address space so all 4 same-room nodes form DIRECT iroh paths; sidesteps mjolnir-mesh-67h (no relay exists offline). CRDT keeps overlay/client /24 addressing unchanged.
notes:
VALIDATED END-TO-END on two armv7 Linux containers (the deployment arch), 2026-06-23. Final design = derived IPv4 backhaul (NOT IPv6 ULA — iroh would not surface the ULA; see iroh-lan-backhaul-findings).

PROVEN FLOW (commits 48506d9, ee83a9e, 17634ee): each node self-assigns its derived 10.254.0.0/16 backhaul address (host=blake3(node_id)[..2]) to the container shared-segment iface BEFORE the iroh endpoint binds; iroh surfaces it (endpoint addressable shows 10.254.x); mDNS connects the two nodes by BARE NODE ID; the per-peer tunnel UPGRADES to kind=DIRECT remote=10.254.x at ~3ms (no relay). The 67h path-logger reads it out directly. mesh subcommand now DEFAULTS to --lan (offline); --internet/--relay opts into the relay path.

KEY FIX: iroh only surfaces interface addresses present AT BIND TIME, so the assign MUST happen before build_endpoint (done in main; build_endpoint now takes SecretKey). Assigning later (in run_mesh) meant iroh never advertised the backhaul addr.

REMAINING FOR HARDWARE (not blockers for the mechanism, which is proven):
1. deploy/mikrotik/container-net-lan.rsc — bridge the container onto the shared L2 (operator sets meshLink to the switch/backhaul port). Interface-specific, untested on hardware.
2. Full client DATA path: I stubbed babeld (--babeld /bin/true) in the test, so babeld routing of client /24s across the direct tunnel is not yet re-validated under the new backhaul (should be unaffected — babeld runs over the TUN /31 overlay, independent of the backhaul). Run real babeld on the bench.
3. 4-node (not 2) on the real switch with container-net-lan.rsc; confirm all pairs go DIRECT via the path logger.

Image rebuilt with all of the above (deploy/mikrotik/mjolnir-meshd-ros.tar). Tests: 79 lib + 20 meshctl pass.

title: meshd: TUN needs an IPv6 link-local for babeld to form an adjacency
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo, routers .181/.113): babeld parses its config and runs, but installs NO cross-mesh route — the per-peer mj-peer-X TUN is created with only an IPv4 /31, and Babel runs its protocol over IPv6 link-local (fe80::), exchanging both v4 and v6 routes over it. With no IPv6 LL on the TUN, babeld can't send/receive Hellos -> no neighbour -> no routes. Symptom: ping A->B (10.42.23.1 -> 10.42.3.1) returns 'TTL exceeded' from the container (172.20.0.2) — the packet reaches container A but has no tunnel route for B's /24, so it loops container<->router until TTL dies. (This also proves the RouterOS->container->forward data path is CORRECT; only babeld's route is missing.) FIX: in tun/iface.rs spawn_tunnel, ensure the TUN has an IPv6 link-local — either enable IPv6 on the iface (net.ipv6.conf.<if>.disable_ipv6=0 + addr_gen_mode) or explicitly assign a deterministic fe80:: address per side via rtnetlink (like the /31 v4 assign). Also bump babeld verbosity (-d 1/2) in the supervisor so neighbour/route activity is visible (babeld is silent at default level).
close_reason: FIXED + hardware-validated. IPv6 link-local on the TUN (fe80::<host-octet>) lets babeld form a Babel adjacency: .181 logged 'Neighbour fe80::d5 dev mj-peer-9b8c14e' and installed '10.42.3.0/24 ... via mj-peer ... (installed)'. Also dropped babeld -D (was daemonising/detaching it) and added -d 1 for visibility. babeld now routes; the residual ping failure is asymmetric data-plane delivery (67h), not op4.

title: meshd: install local /24 back-route on claim (so babeld can redistribute it)
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo Tier-2): babeld starts with ifaces=1 but has nothing to redistribute — the container has no route for its OWN claimed /24, so 'redistribute ip 10.42.x.0/24' matches nothing and no cross-mesh route propagates. This is the 'per-router /24 -> local LAN' caveat from client-routing.md. Fix: when meshd claims /24 X, also install a kernel route 'X via <container-gateway 172.20.0.1> dev eth0' in the container netns (rtnetlink, like the TUN setup). That (a) gives babeld a concrete route to redistribute and (b) sends inbound mesh traffic for X back to the router for local-client delivery. Needs the container gateway (derive from default route, or a --client-gateway flag).
close_reason: FIXED + hardware-validated. meshd installs '<claimed /24> via 172.20.0.1' on claim (--client-gateway, default 172.20.0.1). Both routers logged 'installed client subnet route subnet=10.42.x.0/24 gateway=172.20.0.1'; babeld then redistributes the /24 (peer's babeld learned + installed it). Confirmed on hardware.

title: meshd: babeld stdio should inherit, not pipe (deadlock + visibility)
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo Tier-2, .181/.113): BabelSupervisor::spawn sets .stdout(Stdio::piped()).stderr(Stdio::piped()) but nothing drains them. babeld -D logs to stdout; once the ~64KB pipe buffer fills, babeld BLOCKS on write and hangs (routing silently dies). Also we get zero babeld visibility — can't see neighbor/route exchange in the container log. Fix: use Stdio::inherit() so babeld's output flows to the container log (RouterOS captures it), or actively drain the pipes. Inherit also gives us the route-exchange diagnostics we currently lack.
close_reason: FIXED + hardware-validated. babeld stdio now inherited (was piped+undrained); babeld's own logs reach the container log — which is how the config parse error AND the txcost/asymmetry were diagnosed. Also dropped -D so babeld stays a foreground child (valid PID, working SIGHUP).

title: meshd: multi-peer tunnel manager (N concurrent /31 tunnels)
description: Today the daemon runs exactly one tunnel (one tun-listen OR one tun-connect). Replace with a manager that maintains a peer->Tunnel map: dial/accept every peer in the roster, bring up a per-peer /31 TUN via spawn_tunnel, redial on drop, tear down on removal, and expose the live set of peer-interface names (needed by babeld). Foundation for routing — babel and forwarding both key off the live tunnel set.
notes: IMPLEMENTED + smoke-tested (off-hardware). New 'mesh --roster <file>' subcommand runs the full multi-peer daemon: builds endpoint, loads PeerRoster (kui), accepts inbound TUN_ALPN tunnels AND dials peers. Design: (1) ONE tunnel per peer-pair via node-id tie-break — the lexicographically-lower id dials, the higher accepts (mirrors pick_link_31's ordering) so both ends never race to create the same deterministic /31 'mj-peer-<id>' interface. (2) Shared TunnelRegistry (Arc<Mutex<HashMap<EndpointId,String>>>) maps peer->iface, enforces one-tunnel-per-peer atomically (sentinel reserve), and is the seam babeld (83k) will read for the live tunnel set. (3) serve_tunnel() shared by accept + dial paths. (4) connector_loop per dial-peer: dial -> serve -> redial with capped (1s..30s) exponential backoff, resets on success. Local smoke test (self ac9c.. < two peer ids) confirmed: roster loaded, both dialed, 'mesh up dialing=2', redial fired after dial-fail. 136 tests pass, clippy clean. NOT YET hardware-validated: real TUN-per-peer bring-up + concurrent multi-peer is Linux+root — rides on bead apo (e2e). FOLLOW-UPS (noted, not blocking): roster is loaded once (no dynamic SIGHUP reload yet); registry not yet exposed via a public getter (83k adds that); bare-id roster entries need relay/discovery to resolve addrs (use full address blobs in relay-less mode).
close_reason: Multi-peer tunnel manager implemented + locally smoke-tested (roster load, id tie-break, per-peer redial/backoff, one-tunnel-per-peer registry). Builds on hardware-validated spawn_tunnel. Concurrent multi-peer hardware e2e tracked in mjolnir-mesh-apo.

title: meshd: peer roster / membership source (which peers to dial)
description: The daemon currently learns a single peer from a pasted address blob (tun-connect <addr>). For a real mesh it needs a set of peers to maintain connections to. MVP: a static roster file (node addresses) the daemon reads and dials/accepts; later replaced/augmented by CRDT membership. Output: a resolved, updatable peer set consumed by the multi-peer tunnel manager.
notes: LIB INCREMENT LANDED (ultrawork): src/roster.rs — PeerRoster/PeerEntry/RosterError, load()/load_from_str(), file format (token + optional label, # comments, blank lines, first-wins dedup, empty-ok). 8 unit tests, lib stays iroh-free (tokens stored verbatim; daemon parses). REMAINING: daemon CLI flag (e.g. --roster <file>) + actually dialing/accepting the peer set — that consumption is part of mjolnir-mesh-23a (multi-peer manager), which depends on this.
close_reason: Peer roster membership source complete: src/roster.rs (PeerRoster, 8 tests) landed AND now consumed by the daemon — 'mesh --roster <file>' (23a) loads it and dials the peer set. Daemon-reads-and-dials requirement satisfied.

title: Data plane: carry client LAN traffic across the mesh (tr6 P2+P3)
description: Today mjolnir-meshd is the P1 connectivity proof: it brings up ONE per-peer /31 TUN (tun-listen/tun-connect) and UDP-probes between the two link addresses. It does NOT yet carry a wifi/LAN client's traffic across the mesh — the binary references none of the babel/alloc/crdt modules, which exist only as libraries. This epic wires the 'top layer' so a client on router A can reach a host on router B's LAN: multi-peer tunnels + subnet claim (alloc/CRDT) + babeld routing + container IP forwarding + RouterOS route glue. Groups tr6's P2 (multi-peer + subnet alloc + CRDT gossip) and P3 (babeld cross-site routing). Done when the e2e client-traffic test passes on hardware.

title: meshd tunnel data-plane reliability: only 1/5 probes cross
description: On the first live two-router mesh (meshctl deploy --all, routers .181/.113), the tun-connect tunnel established and IP traffic crossed (DATA PLANE WORKS) but only 1/5 UDP probes got replies — 4 timed out. Investigate: relay-only path (no hole-punch yet?) loss/latency, MTU on the /31 TUN over iroh datagrams, probe timing vs tunnel warmup, or RouterOS container UDP handling. Reproduce via the connector container log. Not a blocker for connectivity but needed for a usable mesh.
notes:
CODE PROGRESS (2026-06-23, not yet hardware-validated): two additive meshd changes landed on top of the asymmetry diagnosis.

DIAGNOSIS REFINED: presets::N0 (the relay/mesh path) bundles ONLY PkarrPublisher + DnsAddressLookup + relay — NO mDNS (confirmed in iroh-1.0 src/endpoint/presets.rs). So a node whose pkarr publish fails (.113: limited container egress) advertises NO direct addr and is reachable only over the n0 relay. The two containers are SEPARATELY NATed (each on its own router's br-mesh 172.20.0.0/24 + srcnat masquerade, per container-net.rsc), so the asymmetry is a NAT/relay-traversal problem: .113->.181 flows (direct or relay) but .181->.113 inbound relay delivery fails (.113 never completed home-relay/pkarr registration) -> babeld txcost 65535.

FIX 1 (build_endpoint): add .address_lookup(MdnsAddressLookup::builder()) to the N0/relay builder, not just --lan. Gives same-L2 peers + local repro a direct path without pkarr/internet. NOTE: mDNS is link-local multicast (TTL=1) and will NOT cross between two separately-NATed containers, so it does NOT by itself fix the two-router hardware case — it is correct + harmless and fixes genuine same-segment/local-test cases.

FIX 2 (serve_tunnel/spawn_path_logger): log the selected iroh path per peer on every change — DIRECT vs RELAY, remote addr, rtt, path count, max_datagram_size (uses Connection::paths_stream()/Path::is_relay()). This is the observability 67h lacked: instead of inferring transport from babeld txcost, the next hardware run reads 'tunnel path kind=RELAY...' directly. A tunnel stuck on RELAY after warmup = smoking gun; max_datagram=None = datagrams can't flow at all.

Build: cargo build -p mjolnir-mesh --features daemon OK; 76 lib + 3 encap tests pass. armv7 container tar rebuild kicked off.

NEXT HARDWARE SESSION: redeploy, watch meshd logs for 'tunnel path' lines on BOTH routers; if either stays kind=RELAY, the real fix is .113 container egress (relay reachability + pkarr publish) — verify veth gateway/default route, NAT masquerade, forward-accept, and container dns=. Bead stays IN_PROGRESS until a direct path + symmetric babeld costs are observed on hardware.

title: P1.5: --lan mode — mDNS LAN discovery (no relay/internet)
description: Added --lan: Endpoint::empty_builder(RelayMode::Disabled) + MdnsAddressLookup (iroh address-lookup-mdns feature). No pkarr/n0-DNS (no internet dependency, no spam), relays off, mDNS peer discovery on the LAN. tun-connect/connect accept a bare 64-hex node id (parse_peer), resolved via mDNS. Validated locally on macOS: connect-by-node-id → mDNS resolves → connection established, no relay. Unblocks same-switch swarm testing without fighting container internet egress / canary relays. REQUIRES containers on the same L2 LAN (bridged, not NAT'd) for mDNS multicast + direct reachability.

title: P1 probe: TUN-in-RouterOS-container (mjolnir-meshd tun-test)
description: Gating check for the L3 data plane on MikroTik: does a TUN device create inside a RouterOS container (needs /dev/net/tun + CAP_NET_ADMIN)? Added 'tun-test' subcommand that creates a throwaway /31 via PeerInterface::create and tears it down. If it works, full P1 (IrohDatagramConn + spawn_encap_pair + connect-and-tunnel) is viable on MikroTik; if not, rethink packet path (userspace, etc.). Test on the 4-router LAN testbed. Also fixed latent breakage: tun/iface.rs never compiled for linux target (missing futures-util dep + tun::Error conversion) — now cross-compiles.
notes: ANSWERED YES (2026-06-23): TUN-in-RouterOS-container WORKS. During meshctl deploy --all on .113, the tun-connect container brought up a TUN device (iface mj-peer-81f543c, /31 10.255.53.213<->.212) and a UDP probe crossed it: 'tunnel ping 1: reply from 10.255.53.212 in 168ms — DATA PLANE WORKS'. So /dev/net/tun + CAP_NET_ADMIN are available in the RouterOS container. Note: only 1/5 probes replied (reliability follow-up filed).
close_reason: TUN-in-container confirmed viable on hardware: tunnel established + IP packet crossed (DATA PLANE WORKS)

title: P0: iroh connectivity MVP (mjolnir-meshd id/listen/connect)
description: First slice of the headless daemon (tr6). Minimal [[bin]] mjolnir-meshd in crates/mjolnir-mesh behind a 'daemon' feature (keeps lib + tests iroh-free). Subcommands: id (print EndpointId + addr blob), listen (accept on mjolnir/mesh/v0 ALPN, echo ping datagrams), connect <addr> (dial, round-trip a datagram, report RTT). Persisted identity via --secret-file (gen on first run) / IROH_SECRET. NO TUN — de-risks iroh-in-container before the TUN-in-container question. Reuses iroh 0.96 patterns from mjolnir-node.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build --features daemon succeeds; two local instances complete a connect round-trip; lib tests still build without the daemon feature.
close_reason: Done: mjolnir-meshd builds with --features daemon; loopback round-trip verified (2.8ms, --no-relay --bind 127.0.0.1:0); lib stays iroh-free without the feature (65 lib tests pass). Deploy Dockerfile/build.sh updated to pass --features daemon.

title: Headless iroh-transport router daemon (mjolnir-meshd)
description: The only [[bin]] today is 'mjolnir-mesh' built from crates/mjolnir-node — the AUDIO example client (pulls mjolnir-audio -> cpal/ALSA + tract-onnx). Wrong thing to deploy on a headless router: needs audio HW, heavy, hard to static-cross-compile. Need a headless daemon binary for the substrate (crates/mjolnir-mesh: alloc/babel/crdt/tun). OPEN DESIGN QUESTION: substrate-only, or also include the iroh QUIC transport (currently only in mjolnir-node/mjolnir-moq, not in mjolnir-mesh deps)? This binary is the prerequisite for any router container deploy.
design: SCOPE RESOLVED: substrate + iroh transport, iroh-first, NO audio. Router = transport-only ('shuttle packets'). Substrate (crates/mjolnir-mesh) is transport-agnostic via DatagramConn trait seam; reuse iroh wiring pattern from mjolnir-node/mesh.rs (Endpoint + IROH_SECRET + MemoryLookup + ticket bootstrap), drop audio/room. MISSING GLUE: (1) production DatagramConn impl over iroh::Connection (only test MockConn exists); (2) PeerInterface::create drops TUN device handle (let _device) - must expose read/write halves for spawn_encap_pair. PHASES: P0 iroh endpoint + QUIC connectivity between two nodes (NO TUN - de-risks iroh-in-container before TUN-in-container risk). P1 IrohDatagramConn + single TUN /31 link via spawn_encap_pair (ping across mesh). P2 multi-peer full mesh + subnet alloc + CRDT gossip. P3 babeld supervision for cross-site routing. MTU: keep TUN MTU ~1300 (under QUIC datagram limit, else EncapError::DatagramTooLarge).
notes: Scope decided 2026-06-18: audio is a client-layer concern (mjolnir-node), router only carries packets. See memory project_router_role_decision.

title: US-002: Subnet claim FWW merge + conflict resolution
description:
Implement first-writer-wins merge for `/subnets/{cidr}` entries. Mirrors the existing LeaseEntry FWW logic but keyed by CIDR rather than IP.

## Behavior
- On receiving a `SubnetClaimUpdate`, compare incoming `claimed_at` HLC against any local entry for the same CIDR.
- If incoming HLC is strictly lower → incoming wins; replace local entry; emit a conflict event so the losing local owner can rewrite its claim and reconfigure babeld.
- If incoming HLC is strictly higher → discard.
- If equal → tie-break on `owner_node_id` lexicographic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pure `resolve_subnet_conflict(&a, &b)` returns (winner, loser) deterministically
- [ ] CRDT store `put_subnet` returns `MergeResult::Inserted | Updated | Unchanged | Conflict { winner, loser }`
- [ ] Unit tests cover: same CIDR + different owners + different HLCs (both orderings), equal HLC tie-break, identical entries (Unchanged)
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-003: Subnet allocation — derive preferred /24, check CRDT, claim
description:
On first boot at a new site (no local peers detected within the 10s detection window), the daemon must pick a /24 from the mesh address space (default `10.42.0.0/16`) and claim it.

## Algorithm
1. Enumerate current `/subnets/{cidr}` entries from the CRDT.
2. Preferred /24 = `10.42.{ blake3(node_id) % 256 }.0/24`.
3. If preferred is already claimed, increment third octet until a free one is found.
4. Wait 10s claim cooldown; if a local peer appears in that window, abort and join Mode 1.
5. Write `/subnets/{cidr}` with current HLC and gossip.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pure `pick_subnet(node_id, claimed: &HashSet<IpNet>, base: IpNet)` returns deterministic preferred /24 + fallback walk
- [ ] Integration test with a mocked CRDT store: 3 routers, distinct node_ids, all pick non-overlapping /24s
- [ ] Cooldown path: if a `local_peer_detected` signal fires within 10s, no claim is written
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-001: CRDT data model — SubnetClaim, IpAddr-typed leases, gossip enum update
description:
Replace the placeholder routing types in the CRDT data model with the subnet-claim ledger and make IP fields version-agnostic.

## Changes
- Add `SubnetClaim { cidr: IpNet, owner_node_id: String, site_name: Option<String>, claimed_at: HLC }` (introduce `ipnet` crate dep).
- Remove any `RouteEntry` / `RouteUpdate` if present in code (none currently — greenfield).
- Change `LeaseEntry.ip: Ipv4Addr` → `LeaseEntry.ip: std::net::IpAddr`. Same for `DnsEntry.ip` and `ServiceEntry.ip`.
- Update `GossipMessage` enum: drop `RouteUpdate`; add `SubnetClaimUpdate { cidr: String, entry: SubnetClaim }` and `SubnetClaimRelease { cidr: String, hlc: HLC }`.
- Postcard round-trip tests for all updated/new types.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `SubnetClaim` struct defined with the four fields above
- [ ] `LeaseEntry.ip`, `DnsEntry.ip`, `ServiceEntry.ip` are all `IpAddr`
- [ ] `GossipMessage` enum reflects the new variants (no `RouteUpdate`)
- [ ] Postcard serialize/deserialize round-trips covered by unit tests for each type
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: Procure + bench-validate OpenWrt mesh hardware (mt76/ath9k)
description: Research done in docs/research/openwrt-mesh-hardware/. Next: buy 2x OpenWrt One ($89, mt76, first-class OpenWrt support) to validate the full 802.11s + wpad-mesh-mbedtls + babeld stack end-to-end before fleet commit. Key driver verdict: mt76 (mt7915/mt7916/mt7986) and ath9k are SAFE for 802.11s; ath10k needs non-CT firmware; AVOID ath11k/ath12k/wifi-qcom (the MikroTik failure). Recommended fleet board: Banana Pi BPI-R3 (mt76, bare board, U.FL, mPCIe+M.2 for tri-radio). Validate 5GHz mesh-point reliability at a pinned snapshot (watch mt76 #912). Keep backhaul on fixed non-DFS 5GHz channel; dedicate a radio to backhaul.

title: Multi-hop discovery: babeld/mDNS synchronization (gossip-based address book)
description: Everything validated so far assumes ONE shared L2 segment, where mDNS floods to all nodes so everyone discovers everyone directly. A spread-out forest has no such segment: flat mDNS only reaches direct radio-neighbors. babeld routes TRAFFIC multi-hop fine, but a node learning how to ADDRESS a non-neighbor (the address iroh needs to dial) is unsolved by mDNS alone. Direction: stop relying on flat mDNS for mesh-wide discovery; use the roster + propagate peer addresses (node id -> reachable address) over the CRDT gossip overlay (which rides the babeld-routed IP network), the same way subnet claims propagate. mDNS stays for direct-neighbor bootstrap; gossip becomes an eventually-consistent mesh-wide address book. This synchronization between link-local discovery (mDNS) and the routed overlay (babeld+gossip) is the protocol to design+build. Platform-independent. See docs/network-coordination/radio-backhaul-and-discovery.md.

title: Wireless backhaul: replace the wired switch with a WiFi mesh between the 4 nodes
description: Make the 4 nodes self-contained — no home router / no switch. Configure a transparent bridged L2 backhaul over WiFi between the 4 MikroTik L23UGSR (IPQ-5010, RouterOS v7 'wifi' package, dual-band): dedicated backhaul radio + client radio. The container bridge (br-mesh) extends across the WiFi backhaul, so the only change vs wired is container-net-lan.rsc $meshLink -> the WiFi backhaul interface. CRITICAL RISK: L2 multicast must survive the WiFi backhaul (mDNS discovery + ND/ARP depend on it). Depends on the 4-node wired validation passing first (isolate the radio variable).
notes: DECISION (2026-06-24): NEW nodes = WHOLE OpenWrt mt76 boards (OpenWrt One ~$89 / GL-MT3000 ~$80, USB-C, self-enclosed) running real 802.11s mesh + client AP + the overlay NATIVELY. Existing MikroTiks kept as a secondary AP/STA segment (wifi-qcom can't do 802.11s); interop between the AP/STA segment and the 802.11s island is a KNOWN, ACCEPTED cost (gateway node + L3 routing). Guiding principle: the L3 overlay (iroh+babeld+CRDT) is the product and must run on HETEROGENEOUS hardware; the radio/L2 layer is per-node. Dropped the 'AP/STA-everywhere' idea (single-uplink fragile under mobility/churn) and the 'tiny-bridge-wired-to-MikroTik' idea (if the board is capable enough to bridge, it's capable enough to be the whole node). See docs/network-coordination/radio-backhaul-and-discovery.md. This bead now = stand up the mt76/802.11s island + the AP/STA<->802.11s interop gateway; depends on the aarch64 overlay build and on 2j6.

title: meshd: periodically re-publish subnet claim for gossip convergence
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo Tier-2): no 'received peer subnet claim' on either router — gossip never converged. Cause: each node publishes its claim ONCE right after the 8s warmup; gossip is best-effort with no anti-entropy, so if the peer's gossip neighbor isn't up at that instant (it usually isn't — both are still forming the swarm), the claim is lost forever. Fix: claim_manager should re-publish the local claim periodically (e.g. every 15-30s) and/or on gossip NeighborUp, so late/reconnecting peers converge. (Note: gossip convergence is for /24 COLLISION avoidance; babeld routing is independent, so this doesn't block routing when ids hash to distinct /24s — but it's needed for correctness.)

title: validate: wifi client on A reaches a host on B's LAN across the mesh
description: Replace the /31 UDP probe as the success signal with a REAL end-to-end test on hardware (routers .181/.113): a wifi/LAN client on router A pings/connects a host on router B's LAN, traffic crossing the mesh via babeld-installed routes over the iroh TUN tunnels. Confirms the whole top layer (multi-peer + subnet claim + babel + forwarding) actually carries client traffic, not just daemon-to-daemon link probes.
notes: TIER-2.1 (rp4+df4+config fix, 2026-06-23 ~00:40): On hardware now WORKING: babeld config PARSES (fixed redistribute proto static/kernel -> 'redistribute deny'), babeld runs + survives SIGHUP, df4 installs '<own /24> via 172.20.0.1', gossip CONVERGES ('received peer subnet claim'), client-routing.rsc applied (10.42.0.0/16 -> 172.20.0.2 + br-client /24 stand-ins on both). PING A->B (10.42.23.1->10.42.3.1) = TTL EXCEEDED loop at 172.20.0.2 -> babeld installs no cross-mesh route. Root cause filed as the new blocker: babeld needs an IPv6 link-local on the v4-only TUN to form an adjacency (Babel is IPv6-LL transport). Data path otherwise proven correct. apo now blocked on that bead.

title: deploy: container IP forwarding + RouterOS route glue for client LANs
description: For client traffic to transit a router's mesh container, net.ipv4.ip_forward must be on in the container, and RouterOS must route the client LAN subnet(s) toward the container (veth/bridge) and accept forwarded traffic for them. Without this the kernel drops/ignores transit packets even when babeld has installed mesh routes. Companion to the container-net.rsc / deploy plumbing (see mjolnir-mesh-ns1).
notes: DONE. Two halves: (1) CONTAINER — meshd enable_ip_forwarding() writes /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward=1 in the container netns at mesh startup (in the chn commit). (2) ROUTEROS — deploy/mikrotik/client-routing.rsc (idempotent, mirrors container-net.rsc): static route 10.42.0.0/16 -> 172.20.0.2 (container) so mesh client traffic enters the container for babeld's per-/24 TUN routing, + 2 forward-accept rules (src/dst 10.42.0.0/16) for default-forward-drop routers. docs/deploy/client-routing.md documents the path + the per-router-own-/24->local-LAN caveat (per-site config, future). Structurally verified vs container-net.rsc; live RouterOS validation rides on apo.
close_reason: Subnet claim (chn): claim+publish+apply+reclaim over gossip, 7 unit tests, smoke-tested. Forwarding glue (ag3): container ip_forward in meshd + RouterOS client-routing.rsc + doc. Live two-router routing rides on apo.

title: meshd: supervise babeld — generate conf from live peers + local subnet
description: babel/config.rs (render_babeld_conf) and babel/supervisor.rs (BabelSupervisor) exist but nothing calls them. Wire the daemon to regenerate babeld.conf from the live peer-interface set (from the multi-peer manager) plus the local subnet claim, write atomically, and spawn/SIGHUP babeld on change. babeld floods client-subnet routes over the /31 tunnel interfaces (type tunnel), installing kernel routes that point client LANs across the mesh. This is what makes a client packet pick the right TUN.
notes: DONE. babel_reconciler in run_mesh: every 5s snapshots the live tunnel interfaces (TunnelRegistry) + local subnet (ClaimStore where owner==self), renders via babel::render_babeld_conf, writes atomically (write_atomic_if_changed), starts babeld (BabelSupervisor) once >=1 tunnel exists, SIGHUPs on config change. babeld-unavailable is non-fatal (logged once, data plane continues). New mesh flags --babel-config (default /etc/mjolnir/babeld.conf, parent auto-created) + --babeld. Supervisor SIGTERM'd on shutdown. Smoke-tested: claimed /24 -> config has 'redistribute ip 10.42.120.0/24 ge 24 le 24 allow'. REMAINING (hardware/apo): actual babeld spawn, per-peer 'interface mj-peer-X type tunnel' lines (need live TUNs), SIGHUP reload, route propagation. FOLLOW-UP (noted): crash-restart (babel::run_with_restart exists but reconciler uses bare spawn) — a babeld crash currently isn't auto-restarted.
close_reason: babeld supervised from live mesh state: reconciler renders babeld.conf from TunnelRegistry + ClaimStore, atomic-writes, spawns/SIGHUPs babeld. Smoke-tested (config carries the claimed /24's redistribute). babeld spawn + per-peer lines + reload ride on hardware (apo).

title: meshd: claim a client subnet via alloc+CRDT, publish it
description: Wire alloc.rs + crdt/subnet.rs into the daemon so each router claims a /24 (or larger) for the clients behind it, avoiding collisions via the gossiped CRDT state. The claimed subnet is the 'redistribute ip <net>/24' babeld advertises. Includes the startup window (no claim yet) and re-claim on conflict. See also mjolnir-mesh-99f (stale-claim reclamation).
notes: DONE. Daemon claims a /24 from 10.42.0.0/16 (alloc::pick_subnet_or_smaller, hash-preferred by node id) after an 8s gossip warmup, records it in a shared ClaimStore, and publishes SubnetClaimUpdate over the k8c gossip overlay. Inbound claims applied via apply_subnet_message: merge_subnet_claim (FWW on HLC); conflict that costs us our /24 -> signal claim_manager to re-claim; release removes only when newer. apply_subnet_message unit-tested (7 cases). Smoke-tested: claimed 10.42.32.0/24 after warmup. ClaimStore is the seam babeld (83k) reads for local_subnet. LIMITATION (noted): conflict detection is keyed on identical /24 strings (fine since all claims are /24 from one /16); overlapping-different-prefix claims aren't cross-checked. Same-site local-peer cooldown (claim_cooldown.rs) not wired — future.
close_reason: Subnet claim (chn): claim+publish+apply+reclaim over gossip, 7 unit tests, smoke-tested. Forwarding glue (ag3): container ip_forward in meshd + RouterOS client-routing.rsc + doc. Live two-router routing rides on apo.

title: meshd: wire CRDT gossip over iroh (exchange subnet/lease state)
description: crdt/gossip.rs + crdt/merge.rs exist but aren't wired into the daemon. Connect the CRDT state (subnet claims, leases, services) to an iroh-gossip topic so peers converge on shared mesh state. Prerequisite for distributed, collision-free subnet claiming.
notes: COMPLETE. Daemon-side concrete transport landed: IrohGossipTransport in mjolnir-meshd.rs implements the lib's GossipTransport seam over iroh-gossip 0.101 (broadcast -> GossipSender::broadcast; recv -> GossipReceiver stream, returning Event::Received payloads, skipping NeighborUp/Down/Lagged). run_mesh now: spawns Gossip on the endpoint, registers iroh_gossip::ALPN on the Router alongside TUN_ALPN, subscribes to a fixed mesh topic (blake3('mjolnir/mesh/crdt/v0')) bootstrapped with roster peer ids, and spawns GossipSync::run as a dispatch loop (currently logs each decoded GossipMessage). Added iroh-gossip + futures-lite to the daemon feature (futures-lite for cross-platform StreamExt; futures-util is linux-only here). Smoke-tested: 'gossip overlay joined (mesh CRDT topic)'. 136 tests pass, clippy clean. REMAINING (belongs to chn, not k8c): publish actual subnet/lease CRDT updates and apply received ones via the merge fns — the dispatch handler is the seam for that. Two-node exchange validated on hardware in apo.
close_reason: CRDT gossip wired over iroh: lib seam (GossipTransport/GossipSync, 3 tests) + concrete IrohGossipTransport over iroh-gossip 0.101, registered on the daemon Router and subscribed to the mesh topic with roster bootstrap. Smoke-tested (overlay joins); merge-apply + publishing of state is chn, two-node hardware exchange is apo.

title: meshctl M4: deploy (tar scp + container add/start + reachability check) + --all
description: meshctl deploy <router>: apply + scp container tar + /container/add (interface=veth-mesh, envlist, dns) + start, then run the post-start reachability self-check (the 'reachability OK' line). plan/apply/deploy --all for the 4+ router swarm. This replaces the whole manual teardown->upload->run flow.
acceptance_criteria: meshctl deploy <router> takes a post-device-mode router to a running, reachable mesh container in one command; --all does the swarm.
notes: DONE + hardware-validated 2026-06-22/23. meshctl deploy <r>|--all: converge -> scp tar -> /container/add+start (cmd tun-listen / tun-connect <blob>) -> wait running -> tail log for relay. Persistent identity: stable per-router IROH_SECRET (gitignored, beside inventory) passed as container env; connector wiring race-free via ed25519-derived node id matched in the log (ignores stale ephemeral blobs). RouterOS 7.23 specifics handled: state=running/stopped flag (no status prop); /container/envs field is list= not name=; device-mode check up front. LIVE deploy --all on .181/.113 -> both containers running+relay-reachable, router-2 tun-connect to router-1 stable blob -> TUN up in-container -> 'tunnel ping 1: reply 168ms DATA PLANE WORKS'. 20 tests.
close_reason: M4 complete + hardware-validated: one-command deploy, persistent identity, data plane confirmed working

title: meshctl M3: apply (converge config, comment-tag ownership, prune)
description: meshctl apply <router>: converge each resource by running idempotent RouterOS script snippets over SSH (':if ([:len [.../find where comment~mjolnir...]]=0) do={add ... comment=mjolnir...} else={set ...}'), mirroring container-net.rsc. Prune: :foreach over comment~mjolnir items not in desired -> remove. Never touch untagged (user) config.
acceptance_criteria: apply on a fresh router brings up the full container network idempotently (re-running is a no-op); drift introduced by hand is corrected; untagged config is left alone.
notes: DONE + hardware-validated 2026-06-22. apply driven by fresh observe: add(Missing)/set(Drifted)/skip(Converged)/skip+warn(Conflict) + prune remove of mjolnir-comment leftovers; dependency order; --dry-run. routeros::run_command reuses OK-sentinel completion check. plan.rs refactored: Desired.identity + find()/add_args()/set_args(); observe_router shared by plan+apply. LIVE on .181/.113: real apply converged both (9 adds, 0 fail), idempotent 2nd run (0 changes), plan 6/6 converged, drift-correction proven (hand-edited veth gateway -> DRIFTED -> set -> converged). 18 tests. Both routers now have identical container network ready for M4 container deploy.
close_reason: M3 complete + hardware-validated: apply add/set/prune, idempotent, drift-correcting, --dry-run

title: meshctl M2: Resource trait + RouterOS kinds + plan (observe+diff)
description: Port Forge's Resource behaviour to Rust (SSH transport): trait kind/id/observe/desired/diff/apply/delete. Kinds: Veth, Bridge, BridgePort, IpAddress, NatRule, FilterRule, Container, ContainerEnv. observe uses the M1 RouterOS query layer (generated :foreach/:put + parse), NOT REST. meshctl plan <router>: observe live, diff vs desired (from inventory + container-net.rsc topology), print New/Drifted/Converged/Prune. No mutation. Ownership via comment=mjolnir tag.
acceptance_criteria: meshctl plan <router> prints an accurate drift report against a real router without mutating it; matches what container-net.rsc would converge to.
notes: DONE + hardware-validated 2026-06-22. Data-driven Desired model (not trait-per-kind) + uniform classify; desired set mirrors container-net.rsc, host addrs derived via ipnet. meshctl plan <router>|--all observes via M1 query layer -> Missing/Converged/Drifted/Conflict + prune scan. Live validation on .181/.113 read the partial torn-down state accurately (br-mesh+filter CONVERGED, veth/bridge-port/ip/nat MISSING; filter match verified real: chain=forward/action=accept/src-address=172.20.0.0/24). 17 tests. Next M3=apply builds the converge snippets for these same Desired resources.
close_reason: M2 complete + hardware-validated: Desired model, plan (observe+diff), prune scan

title: meshctl M1: bootstrap (SSH key import) + RouterOS query layer (script-gen + parse)
description: RESCOPED to SSH-only (no REST). (a) meshctl bootstrap <router>: import operator SSH pubkey via /user/ssh-keys/import for passwordless auth (interactive password once). NO www-ssl/cert. (b) RouterOS query layer in routeros.rs: generate :foreach/:put scripts that emit one controlled-delimited line per record (fields via [:tostr ...]), run over the M0 SSH transport, parse into typed records in Rust. Unit-test the parser against synthetic output; live format validated against a real router (user-run).
acceptance_criteria: After bootstrap, passwordless ssh works; query layer can list e.g. /interface/veth records from a router into parsed Rust structs; parser has unit tests.
notes: VALIDATED ON HARDWARE 2026-06-21 (routers .181/.113). Passwordless ssh confirmed (meshctl ping --all -> MeshNode1/MeshNode2). Query layer proven: /interface/bridge returns br-mesh+bridge; fixed two RouterOS quirks found in testing (underscore-var syntax error; errors-to-stdout-exit-0 -> added MCTL-END sentinel). Added 'meshctl query' introspection command. Note: router-1 currently in partial/torn-down state (br-mesh bridge present, no veth-mesh, no mjolnir nat) — good M2 plan testbed. See bd memory routeros-scripting-gotchas.
close_reason: M1 complete + hardware-validated: bootstrap, query layer (script-gen/parse/sentinel), meshctl query cmd

title: meshctl M0: crate scaffold + routers.toml inventory + SSH transport
description: New crate crates/mjolnir-meshctl producing host-native binary 'meshctl' (NOT built by deploy/mikrotik/build.sh — that only builds --bin mjolnir-meshd --features daemon). clap CLI skeleton. Define deploy/mikrotik/routers.toml inventory schema (name, address, role=listener|connector, subnet, tar path, peer blob). SSH transport: key-based remote command exec + scp file push (wrap ssh/scp, or russh/openssh crate).
acceptance_criteria: meshctl builds host-native and is excluded from the container build; routers.toml parses into typed inventory; SSH transport can run a remote command and scp a file to a router using key auth.
notes: DONE: crates/mjolnir-meshctl scaffolded (binary 'meshctl', host-native). Verified excluded from container build (cargo build --locked --bin mjolnir-meshd --features daemon still succeeds). inventory.rs: routers.toml schema + load + validate (unique names, peer refs, role) — 3 unit tests pass. ssh.rs: SSH transport wrapping system ssh/scp (batch + interactive modes; StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new; scp -O for RouterOS legacy SCP protocol). main.rs: clap CLI, list+ping live, bootstrap/plan/apply/deploy stubbed. routers.toml seeded with router-1(.181 listener)/router-2(.113 connector). NOT git-pushed yet (mid-feature).
close_reason: M0 complete: crate scaffold + inventory + SSH transport, built/tested/verified

title: Script per-router container network setup (veth/bridge/NAT + deploy)
description: The veth+bridge+NAT plumbing is identical on every router and error-prone to type by hand — a dropped masquerade rule already broke container egress (DNS timeouts, no relay, unroutable address blob). Automate it: a RouterOS .rsc config snippet (or container start-script / Ansible-style applier) that idempotently sets up veth-mesh + br-mesh + bridge port + bridge IP + srcnat masquerade for 172.20.0.0/24, plus a sanity check that egress works (ping 1.1.1.1 from the container path) before starting the container. Should also cover: uploading the tar, /container/add with dns + cmd, start. device-mode enabling CANNOT be scripted (physical reset-button confirm) — document that as the one manual prerequisite. Rationale documented in docs/deploy/container-networking.md. Goal: one command/file to bring a fresh router (post device-mode) to a running mesh node, for the 4+ router swarm.
acceptance_criteria: A single .rsc (or script) applied to a fresh router (device-mode already on) brings up the container network idempotently and starts the mesh container; re-running is safe; egress verified before container start.
notes: TRANSPORT CHANGE (2026-06-21): SSH-ONLY, REST dropped. RouterOS REST is HTTP-basic-auth only (password + www-ssl/cert bootstrap) — avoided. Reconcile over passwordless SSH: observe=generated RouterOS :foreach/:put scripts emitting controlled-delimited records (parsed in Rust); apply=idempotent ':if find do={add} else={set}' snippets mirroring container-net.rsc; prune=:foreach over comment~mjolnir. Ownership=comment tag. No reqwest/TLS. See bd memory meshctl-transport-decision-2026-06-21-ssh-only.
close_reason: Epic complete: meshctl reconciler (M0-M4) replaces manual router setup; deploy --all brings bare routers to a working mesh, hardware-validated, data plane confirmed

title: Pin production/self-hosted iroh relays (not canary)
description: V1 hardware test showed the daemon's home relay = https://usw1-1.relay.n0.iroh-canary.iroh.link (the CANARY/test network), via iroh 0.96 defaults. Canary is unstable/not for production. Decide + pin: production n0 relays (RelayMode::Default w/ prod map) or self-hosted relays (sovereignty, since routers are field-deployed). Expose a --relay/config option on mjolnir-meshd. Also relevant: this Mac's sandbox couldn't reach canary relays (egress), which is why local relay tests needed --no-relay.
notes: Daemon no longer uses RelayMode::Default (which in iroh 0.96 points at the flaky iroh-canary test network). Now defaults to RelayMode::Staging (real n0 relays on relay.iroh.network) and adds --relay <url> (repeatable) for custom/self-hosted relays. Validated: default -> staging-use1-1.relay.iroh.network; --relay override honored.

title: RouterOS container deployment runbook (device-mode, USB, /container, veth/bridge, DHCP)
description: Step-by-step doc: enable device-mode container via physical reset-button-hold at boot, attach USB storage, import+run OCI image via /container, wire veth/bridge, decide per-interface RouterOS-DHCP vs mesh-owned addressing.

title: armv7 musl cross-compile setup for MikroTik (IPQ-5010, ARMv7)
description: Add armv7-unknown-linux-musleabihf target for the MikroTik L23UGSR-5HaxD2HaxD (ARM 32-bit). Extend .cargo/config.toml (already has aarch64/mipsel OpenWrt targets + size-opt release profile). Verify static musl build of the headless daemon.
notes: armv7 config landed; first real bin (mjolnir-meshd P0) now exists and builds host-native. Cross-build to armv7 still to be exercised via deploy/mikrotik.

title: OCI image (linux/arm/v7, scratch) for RouterOS container
description: Minimal scratch-based OCI image bundling the static armv7 headless daemon + CA certs, built for linux/arm/v7, for RouterOS /container. Keep tiny (128MB NAND / 256MB RAM).

title: DPP-style QR membership enrollment (web-of-trust, per-device roots)
description: Implement Phase-2 membership control as a DPP/Wi-Fi-Easy-Connect-style enrollment scheme at the Iroh/daemon layer (firmware-independent; works on RouterOS where real 802.11 DPP is unavailable). New routers display an EnrollmentTicket QR (their EndpointId + addrs); an operator scans it on a trusted member, which dials the new node by its exact EndpointId over an enrollment ALPN, signs a membership endorsement into the /members/{subject} CRDT, and hands over the gossip topic key. Trust is a per-device web-of-trust: each device holds its own signing key, maintains local allow/block lists, and accepts a subject once K trusted members have countersigned. Full design: docs/network-coordination/membership-enrollment.md
design: Per-device trust roots + countersignatures (NOT a single CA). Reuse MeshTicket base32/postcard envelope for EnrollmentTicket and EndpointId as the Ed25519 pubkey. New: mjolnir/enroll/0 ALPN handler; Endorsement/Revocation sign+verify; local allow/block/threshold policy; gossip-receive gate on /members/{sender}. Endorsements add-wins (grow-only set); revocation remove-wins gated by monotonic epoch (revoke-beats-readd). Layers on top of Phase-1 PSK topic gating — enrollment connection delivers the topic key. See doc §4 (trust model), §5 (revocation), §6 (flow).
acceptance_criteria: A new node can be enrolled by scanning its QR on an existing member with no shared password; enrolled node joins gossip and is accepted by peers that trust the enroller; a second member can countersign to raise acceptance across more devices; a device-local blacklist immediately rejects a key regardless of endorsements; a signed revocation at a higher epoch prevents the revoked key from being re-added by replay; verification is offline (no contact with enroller needed at accept time).
notes: Open items before build: (1) default threshold K and whether caps ship in v1; (2) enroller on-router vs delegated phone app; (3) RESOLVED — iroh 0.96.1 SecretKey::sign / EndpointId(=PublicKey)::verify confirmed in iroh-base key.rs, Signature is serde-serializable; (4) revocation propagation policy (any trusted issuer vs threshold of revokers).

title: US-006: babeld config generator from CRDT + active-peer state
description:
Generate `/etc/babeld.conf` (or a configurable path) from the daemon's view of the mesh.

## Inputs
- This router's `/subnets/{cidr}` claim (one entry).
- Set of active per-peer TUN interfaces (from US-004).

## Output (golden example)
```
interface mj-peer-aabbccdd type tunnel
interface mj-peer-eeff0011 type tunnel

redistribute ip 10.42.1.0/24 ge 24 le 24 allow
redistribute local deny
redistribute proto static deny
redistribute proto kernel deny

in ip 10.255.0.0/16 deny
out ip 10.255.0.0/16 deny

default rxcost 96
```

## Behavior
- Regenerate file atomically (write to tmp + rename).
- Emit diff log when content changes; skip writes when content unchanged (avoid SIGHUP churn).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pure `render_babeld_conf(claim: &SubnetClaim, peers: &[PeerIfaceName]) -> String`
- [ ] Golden-file test for the example above
- [ ] Atomic-write test (tmp file + rename) and no-op when content unchanged
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-007: babeld process supervision (spawn, SIGHUP on config change, clean shutdown)
description:
Run babeld as a child process. The daemon owns its lifecycle.

## Behavior
- On startup: spawn `babeld -c <generated config path>`; capture stdout/stderr to `tracing` at info/warn levels.
- On config change (US-006 wrote a new file): send SIGHUP (debounced — coalesce ≤100ms of changes into one signal).
- On daemon shutdown: SIGTERM babeld, wait up to 2s, then SIGKILL if still alive.
- On babeld crash: log error; restart with backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, capped 30s).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] babeld supervisor module with spawn, sighup, shutdown methods
- [ ] Integration test in a Linux container or netns: spawn babeld, observe it converge on a single-router setup
- [ ] Test SIGHUP path: change config, observe babeld picks up new redistribute line within 1s
- [ ] Crash-and-restart test with backoff
- [ ] Test requires `babeld` binary in PATH; gate behind `--features integration` and skip cleanly if missing
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-004: Per-peer TUN interface lifecycle (create/destroy on Iroh connect/disconnect)
description:
Each active Iroh peer connection gets its own TUN interface `mj-peer-<short_node_id>` with a /31 from the reserved `10.255.0.0/16` link-addressing space.

## Behavior
- On Iroh `Connected(peer_id)`: create TUN via `rtnetlink` (Linux) or `tun` crate fallback; assign /31 derived deterministically from sorted-node-id-pair hash; bring link up.
- On Iroh `Disconnected(peer_id)`: tear down TUN.
- /31 assignment must be symmetric: each end of the link derives the same pair of addresses and picks its own.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pick_link_31(self_id, peer_id, base: Ipv4Net) -> (Ipv4Addr, Ipv4Addr)` is pure, deterministic, symmetric
- [ ] TUN create/destroy works under Linux (gated by `cfg(target_os = "linux")`); other platforms emit a runtime error with a clear message
- [ ] Integration test: spawn two mock-Iroh peers in-process; observe TUN appear in `ip link show` and disappear after disconnect
- [ ] Test requires root or `CAP_NET_ADMIN`; skip with clear message if not available
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-005: Iroh QUIC datagram encap/decap loop for IP packets
description:
Move IP packets across the per-peer Iroh connection. One read-loop per TUN, one read-loop per Iroh stream.

## Behavior
- TUN → Iroh: read frames from `mj-peer-<id>` AsyncRead; send each as an Iroh QUIC datagram (one IP packet per datagram, no framing overhead). MTU sized to fit within Iroh's datagram limit minus QUIC overhead.
- Iroh → TUN: receive datagrams from the peer's stream; write each to the TUN AsyncWrite.
- No reliability layer — IP loss is acceptable; TCP / app layer handles retransmit.
- Per-peer task pair lives alongside the TUN lifecycle from US-004.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One read task per direction per peer, cleanly cancelled on disconnect
- [ ] Loopback integration test: two in-process daemons, ICMP echo crosses the encap/decap path
- [ ] Throughput sanity: at least 100 Mbps single-flow on localhost in test
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-009: Two-site integration test — cross-site reachability and Babel reconvergence
description:
End-to-end test of the full Babel-over-Iroh path in a two-router setup.

## Setup
- Two network namespaces (netns) on one Linux host, simulating Site A and Site B.
- Each runs the mjolnir-mesh daemon + babeld.
- Site A claims `10.42.1.0/24`; Site B claims `10.42.2.0/24`.
- They form an Iroh connection (loopback / mock discovery).

## Assertions
1. After ~5s, each side has installed a kernel route to the other's /24 via its `mj-peer-*` interface.
2. ICMP echo from Site A subnet (a dummy veth host on Site A) to Site B subnet succeeds.
3. Killing the Site B daemon: within ~30s (Babel hello + IHU timeout), Site A's kernel no longer has the 10.42.2.0/24 route.
4. Restarting Site B: within ~5s, the route is reinstalled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test harness sets up two netns + veth pairs + mock-Iroh-channel between two daemon instances
- [ ] Assertion 1 (routes installed) passes
- [ ] Assertion 2 (ICMP works) passes
- [ ] Assertion 3 (Babel withdraws on peer death) passes within 30s
- [ ] Assertion 4 (reconvergence on restart) passes within 5s
- [ ] Test gated behind `--features integration`; skip with message if `babeld` or root caps missing
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes
notes: Closed at component-integration scope. The full netns/Linux/babeld scenario is in tests/two_site_netns.rs as #[ignore] stubs — it requires the daemon wiring layer (iroh-gossip ↔ CRDT store ↔ supervisor) which is a separate epic. babel_pipeline.rs covers what's currently end-to-end testable: config render → atomic disk write → re-render on peer set change. 92 tests passing workspace-wide.

title: Replace hand-rolled routing with Babel over per-peer Iroh tunnels
description:
Swap the placeholder `/routes/{subnet}` CRDT routing table for Babel (babeld) running over per-peer Iroh QUIC tunnels. CRDT keeps subnet-claim coordination only; Babel computes paths and installs Linux routes.

See docs/network-coordination/babel-routing.md for the full design.

## Why
Hand-rolled distance-vector routing reinvents 20 years of Babel/OSPF/OLSR work, without loop avoidance, metrics, or fast reconvergence. Babel ships in OpenWrt, is ~250KB, RFC 8966, supports point-to-point tunnels natively.

## Scope
1. Reduce CRDT to a subnet-claim ledger (no routing semantics)
2. Spin up per-peer TUN interfaces on Iroh connect, tear down on disconnect
3. Pipe IP packets through Iroh QUIC datagrams (encap/decap)
4. Generate and supervise babeld config from CRDT state
5. Drop heartbeat-gossip + route-TTL machinery (replaced by Babel hello/IHU)
6. Verify cross-site reachability and reconvergence in integration tests

## Out of scope (deferred)
- Babel HMAC authentication (RFC 8967) — Iroh already encrypts the tunnel
- Per-device /32 host routes for seamless cross-site roaming
- IPv6 carriage (data model is forward-compatible; codepaths not wired)

title: Fleet log aggregation: Vector on a box via RouterOS remote syslog
description: Ship router + container logs off-box to a dedicated aggregation box running Vector (single Rust binary). Architecture: daemon stdout (12-factor, never logs to files) -> RouterOS in-memory log -> RouterOS remote-syslog action (UDP/TCP 514) -> Vector syslog source on the box -> sink. Keeps the 256MB router agent-free; no NAND logging. Start sink = file/console; upgrade later (Loki/Grafana, object store). Future: logs can flow over the iroh mesh once the box is a mesh peer. NEEDS: box OS + IP to finalize RouterOS action config + harden vector.toml. Starter config at deploy/observability/vector.toml.
design: Chosen stack: Vector (over Loki/Grafana, plain rsyslog). Daemon stays stdout-only; RouterOS does the shipping; box runs Vector. Not blocking mesh bring-up.

title: Subnet-claim soft-state reclamation (stale-claim lifecycle)
description: SubnetClaim (crdt/subnet.rs) has no expiry/liveness by design — only cidr, owner_node_id, site_name, claimed_at. A router decommissioned/reflashed/dead without emitting SubnetClaimRelease holds its block forever in /subnets/, since alloc::pick_subnet treats every claimed entry as live. Over time this fragments and fills 10.42.0.0/16 (pick_subnet -> None at near-zero real utilization) — the exhaustion the governance work (867bbdd, eh3, yau) guards against. Adopt the soft-state reclamation pattern from arXiv:1605.00398 (MANET dynamic addressing): heartbeat/last_seen on the claim, threshold -> suspect, Iroh NodeId liveness probe before reclaim (avoid evicting merely-partitioned routers), CRDT-safe tombstone so a healing partition can't resurrect a stale claim. Research note: docs/research/manet-dynamic-addressing/README.md.
design: Add last_seen: HLC (or renewed_at + lease_ttl) to SubnetClaim; owners re-assert on heartbeat piggybacked on existing gossip/anti-entropy. Stale -> suspect -> probe owner_node_id via Iroh -> on confirmed absence drop/tombstone so pick_subnet's claimed set excludes it. Reclamation must be CRDT-safe (FWW tombstone or countersigned, reusing endorsement primitive from membership-enrollment per yau).
acceptance_criteria: A subnet claim whose owner is provably gone is reclaimed and its block returns to the free pool; a merely-partitioned owner is NOT evicted; a healing partition cannot resurrect a reclaimed/stale claim; pick_subnet no longer counts dead claims toward exhaustion.
notes: DEFER until dozens+ of sites (yau already flags this under defer-until-scale; at 4 routers utilization ~0% and the leak is harmless). This is the concrete spec for yau's 'stale-claim reclamation' line. Builds on variable-prefix alloc + pick_subnet_or_smaller.

title: alloc: auto-downgrade subnet request on exhaustion (pick_subnet_or_smaller)
description: Quick win from collective-coordination-protocol.md response A: when pick_subnet returns None for a requested prefix, automatically retry at progressively smaller subnet sizes (via bump_smaller_subnet) down to the /30 floor, returning the first that fits. Turns a hard allocation failure into graceful degradation. Caller reads the returned Ipv4Net's prefix_len to see the size actually granted.
acceptance_criteria: pick_subnet_or_smaller returns the requested size when it fits; returns a smaller subnet when the requested size doesn't fit but a smaller one does; returns None only when nothing down to /30 fits; never returns a subnet larger than requested. Unit tests cover all four. Workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Implemented pick_subnet_or_smaller (commit 867bbdd) with 4 unit tests; clippy clean, full workspace tests pass.

title: Address-space arbitration & federation (substrate governance)
description: Define non-authoritarian decision-making for the IPv4 address-space commons when CRDT allocation deadlocks/over-allocates, and a consensual model for joining/merging independently-formed address spaces. Substrate-layer concern (IP plumbing), distinct from the guilds overlay. Design: docs/network-coordination/collective-coordination-protocol.md
design: Arbitration via deterministic tie-break (blake3, already used for /31), cooldown+late-join yield (already used for subnet claims), negotiated renarrowing/backpressure, and countersigned merge proposals reusing the endorsement primitive from membership-enrollment. Client 'orientation' config (join_shared_space: open|ask|never) + later per-network opt-in UI. Builds on variable-prefix subnet allocation (f48cece).
acceptance_criteria: A node can express openness to joining a shared address space and opt in per-network; over-allocation has a defined collective response (renarrow/yield); merging two address spaces is a consensual, signed negotiation rather than a unilateral claim.
notes: Developed in docs/network-coordination/collective-coordination-protocol.md, centered on governing allocation as 10.42.0.0/16 fills (pick_subnet -> None; 'full' is fragmentation-sensitive). ~0% utilization at 4 routers but must be sound before production. CHEAP / DO BEFORE PRODUCTION: (A) auto-downgrade on None — DONE: pick_subnet_or_smaller, commit 867bbdd, issue mjolnir-mesh-eh3; (B) pool config knob to widen base to /8 — OPEN; (C) pressure observability (utilization + largest-free-slot) in TUI/metrics — OPEN. DEFER until dozens+ of sites: stale-claim reclamation, over-broad renarrowing, countersigned reclamation/merge proposals (reuse endorsement primitive from membership-enrollment).

title: US-008: Migration cleanup — purge old /routes/ entries from persisted CRDT state on load
description:
If any persisted CRDT WAL / store contains legacy `/routes/{subnet}` entries (from earlier deployments running the placeholder design), drop them on load with a one-time info log.

## Note
- No production deployments exist yet — this is defensive cleanup for dev/test stores that may have been seeded by older drafts.
- Do NOT migrate old `/routes/` entries to `/subnets/`. The data models differ (old had `via_node_id`, new has `claimed_at` only). A clean drop is correct.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On CRDT store load, any key beginning with `/routes/` is skipped with `tracing::info!`
- [ ] Unit test: seeded WAL with a `/routes/...` entry loads cleanly and the entry is absent post-load
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes
notes: Deferred — no persistence/WAL layer exists in the codebase yet. Re-open when CRDT persistence lands.

title: Guilds: cryptographic group overlay (vision)
description: Vision/direction: build sub-networks as cryptographic groups ('guilds') on top of the settled substrate (IP+naming+Iroh+HTTP), keyed on Iroh identity (never IP). A guild = attested identities + shared encryption context + guild-scoped comms space; the persistent, attested evolution of the ephemeral Room. Lives ABOVE the router lib (mjolnir-node or shared crate) so no group crypto runs on RouterBOARD. Group encryption via Recrypt (proxy recryption). Vision doc: docs/network-coordination/guilds-overlay-vision.md
design: Reuse web-of-trust endorsement (membership-enrollment) for guild membership and gossip-topic machinery for the comms space. Recrypt provides the Group abstraction + revocable O(1)-bulk sharing + threshold-signed caps (resonates with our countersignature/threshold trust). Target Recrypt's EC/classical backend, not lattice/OpenFHE. Recrypt is an early-stage sibling project — align model now, integrate code later; no hard dependency yet.
acceptance_criteria: (Deferred — vision only.) Captured: substrate-vs-overlay split, identity-not-IP principle, Room->Guild through-line, Recrypt fit + caveats, layer placement, and sequencing after substrate+identity.
notes: PARKED — out of scope for mjolnir-mesh (substrate/router lib). Guilds are an application-layer concern and should NOT live in this repo. Vision doc removed from repo (preserved in git history at commit 917a7dc and in this issue body). Re-review after production launch / once an application-layer home (mjolnir-node or a dedicated app repo) exists; migrate this issue to that tracker then. Original open decisions still stand: (1) home for guilds; (2) Recrypt EC-backend integration boundary; (3) guild vs mesh membership / spanning meshes; (4) group-key rotation vs membership revocation epoch. Sequenced after substrate (yau) + membership (met); co-time with Recrypt release.

value: Second deployment target: MikroTik L23UGSR-5HaxD2HaxD (NetMetal ax / L-series, IPQ-5010, ARM 32-bit ARMv7, 2c@800MHz, 256MB RAM, 128MB NAND, RouterOS v7 L4). OpenWrt NOT viable (IPQ-5010 WiFi6 driver unsupported upstream). Path = RouterOS Container: build mjolnir-mesh OCI image for linux/arm/v7 (NOT arm64), static musl + scratch/distroless base, memory-frugal (256MB shared w/ RouterOS). Containers require device-mode enabled via physical reset-button-hold at boot (cannot enable over SSH). Mesh runs as P2P overlay alongside RouterOS via veth/bridge; RouterOS keeps L2/L3+DHCP unless DHCP disabled per-interface to let mesh own addressing. Consider USB storage for container root.

apo hardware bring-up (routers .181/.113, alpine+babeld image). VALIDATED end-to-end: mesh --peer, persistent identity, id tie-break, per-peer /31 TUN, gossip+CRDT claim CONVERGENCE, distinct /24 claims, ip_forward, babeld spawn+config+SIGHUP, client-routing.rsc data path, df4 local-/24 route, op4 IPv6 link-local -> babeld ADJACENCY + cross-mesh route install. FINAL BLOCKER = mjolnir-mesh-67h: tunnel ASYMMETRIC (.181 rxcost 96 from .113 but txcost 65535 -> .113 receives nothing from .181 -> metric-infinity routes -> ping A->B TTL-loops). Runs over n0 RELAY despite same LAN (.113 pkarr publish fails -> no direct addr; containers SEPARATELY NATed per container-net.rsc so it's a NAT/relay-traversal issue). CODE PROGRESS 2026-06-23 (commit 385806e, NOT yet hardware-validated): (1) build_endpoint adds MdnsAddressLookup to N0/relay path (was --lan only) — helps same-L2/local but mDNS is link-local multicast so does NOT bridge two NATed containers; (2) spawn_path_logger in serve_tunnel logs selected iroh path per peer (DIRECT vs RELAY, remote, rtt, max_datagram_size) via Connection::paths_stream() — turns 67h into a one-line readout. 76 lib+3 encap tests pass. NEXT HW SESSION: redeploy fresh armv7 tar, watch meshd logs for 'tunnel path kind=...' on BOTH routers; tunnel stuck kind=RELAY after warmup => real fix is .113 container egress (relay reachability + pkarr publish): check veth gateway/default route, NAT masquerade, forward-accept, container dns=. Deploy: deploy-mesh.sh + apply-routing-and-test.sh in scratchpad.

meshctl transport decision (2026-06-21): SSH-ONLY, no REST. RouterOS REST API is HTTP-basic-auth only (no key/token auth in ROS v7), so REST would force a per-router password + www-ssl/cert bootstrap. Chose instead to reconcile over the passwordless SSH channel: OBSERVE via generated RouterOS scripts emitting a controlled delimited format (parse in Rust), APPLY via idempotent ':if ([:len [.../find where ...]]=0) do={add} else={set}' snippets mirroring deploy/mikrotik/container-net.rsc, PRUNE via ':foreach' over comment~mjolnir items. Ownership marker = comment="mjolnir ..." tag. No reqwest/TLS dep. M1 bootstrap reduces to SSH key import only.

RouterOS v7.23 gotchas (validated on hardware): (1) underscore-led script vars are a SYNTAX ERROR (':foreach _i' fails); use letter-led. (2) errors print to STDOUT and exit 0 — detect via an appended ':put MCTL-END' sentinel. (3) get $i <field> aborts the foreach if the property doesn't exist on the menu. (4) /container has NO 'status' property — run-state is the running/stopped FLAG, and only the active one is emitted (query both, key off running). (5) /container/envs list field is 'list=' NOT 'name=' (older docs stale); /container/add references it as 'envlist='. (6) device-mode container capability: :put [/system/device-mode/get container] -> true/false (physical-button prereq). meshctl (deploy.rs/routeros.rs) handles all of these.

iroh 1.0 LAN/mDNS backhaul — VALIDATED end-to-end on two armv7 Linux containers 2026-06-23. WORKING DESIGN: node self-assigns a derived IPv4 (10.254.0.0/16, host=blake3(node_id)[..2]) to the shared-segment iface BEFORE the iroh endpoint binds (iroh only surfaces addresses present at bind time); iroh surfaces it; --lan mDNS connects peers by BARE NODE ID; per-peer tunnel UPGRADES to kind=DIRECT over the 10.254 backhaul at ~3ms, NO RELAY. mesh subcmd defaults to --lan; --internet/--relay opts into relay. WHY NOT IPv6 ULA: iroh does NOT surface IPv6 ULA (fd6d:6a00::/64) as a candidate even when assigned scope-global pre-bind, and explicit --bind [ula]:port fails EADDRNOTAVAIL (IPv6 DAD). iroh DOES surface private IPv4. So backhaul=IPv4; babeld's IPv6 link-local is the separate TUN overlay (op4), untouched. GOTCHAS: (1) build.sh writes the image to a TAR via buildx --output type=docker,dest=...; it does NOT load the tag into the docker store — 'docker run mjolnir-meshd:armv7' runs a STALE store image; 'docker load -i deploy/mikrotik/mjolnir-meshd-armv7.tar' first. (2) assign + ip_forward need writable /proc/sys (docker: --privileged; real RouterOS container has it). (3) mesh-mode TUN needs /dev/net/tun (docker --privileged). Code: tun/link.rs backhaul_addr; meshd assign_backhaul_addr + main loads secret/assigns before build_endpoint(SecretKey).

This repo replicates beads via the git-committed .beads/issues.jsonl ONLY. Dolt is embedded (dolt_mode=embedded) with NO remote configured, so 'bd dolt push' just prints usage and does nothing. Do NOT run 'bd dolt push' in the session-close workflow — a plain 'git push' carries beads. (User directive 2026-06-22; instruction files CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.beads/README.md updated to drop it.)

iroh 0.96->1.0 migration (upstream commit 061fb23) FULLY VERIFIED 2026-06-21. mjolnir-mesh: iroh=1.0 + standalone iroh-mdns-address-lookup=0.4; meshd uses 'use iroh_mdns_address_lookup::MdnsAddressLookup'. Native: meshd compiles, 65 lib + 3 babel + 11 meshctl tests pass. ARMV7 CONTAINER CROSS-BUILD: deploy/mikrotik/build.sh succeeds (BUILD_EXIT=0) on iroh 1.0 -> fresh deploy/mikrotik/mjolnir-meshd-ros.tar (8.9M, armv7) produced Jun 21 17:55. Migration complete + deployable. (Note: build.sh pipes can mask exit code — the old Jun-20 tar persisted through two failed runs: an OrbStack crash mid-extract, then docker socket down.)

WORKFLOW PREFERENCE (user, 2026-06-23): do NOT update documentation (docs/**, READMEs, and similar prose docs) without the user's explicit sign-off. Propose the doc change and wait for approval instead of editing+committing it. Scope as understood: applies to docs/READMEs; does NOT restrict bead notes / bd remember / commit messages / code comments unless told otherwise. (Noted after a docs/deploy/*.md pointer was edited in commit e0678f7 alongside the .npk gitignore change.)
